facts.text: Madrid's first central bank began as the Bank of San Carlos in 1782, later becoming the Bank of Spain in 1856.
Between January 7 and 9, 2021, Madrid received 50 to 60 cm of snow, the most ever recorded since 1904.
Madrid hosts the headquarters of RTVE, Atresmedia, Mediaset España, and Movistar+, making it a global TV and film production center.
Founded in 1902, Real Madrid plays La Liga matches at Santiago Bernabéu Stadium, one of the world's largest football arenas.
The Madrid Dome in Antarctica's Aristotle Mountains is named after Spain's capital, linking the city to polar geography.
Madrid's Golden Triangle of Art on Paseo del Prado unites the Prado, Reina Sofía, and Thyssen-Bornemisza museums in one cultural corridor.
